<!-- GENERATED by scripts/gen-reason-codes.ts from src/reasons.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

# Reason codes

Every decision the guard makes carries a stable machine `reason` code. When a payment is denied —
or a policy won't load — this table says what the code means and what to change. Codes are grouped
by *where you encounter them*. This file is generated from the guard's reason registry, so it can
never drift from the codes actually emitted.

## At policy load

You see these while loading or parsing your policy file (before any payment).

| Code | What it means | What to change |
|------|---------------|----------------|
| `config.allowlist_invalid` | policy.allowlist is not an array of { address, chain } entries. | Provide an array; each entry needs a valid address and CAIP-2 chain. |
| `config.cap_key_malformed` | A caps key is not a valid chain\|token coordinate. | Use "chain\|token", e.g. "eip155:8453\|0x…"; build it with assetKey(). |
| `config.caps_invalid` | policy.caps is malformed (not an object, or an entry lacks perRequest/perDomain/global). | Each denomination needs perRequest, perDomain, and global base-unit caps. |
| `config.decimals_invalid` | A display `decimals` is not a non-negative integer. | decimals is the token's base-unit exponent (e.g. 6 for USDC) — a small whole number. |
| `config.dir_world_writable` | The policy file's directory is world-writable; refusing to load it. | Tighten the directory mode (a world-writable dir lets the file be swapped). |
| `config.display_invalid` | The optional `display` section is malformed (not an object, or an entry/key is bad). | display maps a `chain\|token` key to { decimals, symbol }; it is display-only and never affects enforcement. |
| `config.file_unreadable` | The policy file (or its directory) could not be stat'd/read. | Check the path and permissions. |
| `config.halt_invalid` | policy.halt is not a boolean. | Set halt to true or false. |
| `config.json_malformed` | The policy file is not valid JSON. | Fix the JSON syntax. |
| `config.not_an_object` | The policy is not a JSON object. | The policy file must be a single JSON object. |
| `config.require_origin_match_invalid` | policy.requireOriginMatch is not a boolean. | Set requireOriginMatch to true or false. |
| `config.symbol_invalid` | A display `symbol` is not a string. | symbol is a display-only label (e.g. "USDC"); it never affects a decision. |
| `config.world_writable` | The policy file is world-writable; refusing to load it. | Tighten the file mode (a world-writable policy is untrusted). |
| `parse.address_malformed` | An address is not a 20-byte 0x address. | Replace the placeholder/typo with a valid 0x… payee address. |
| `parse.amount_malformed` | An amount is not a non-negative integer. | Use a base-unit integer (decimal string or bigint) — no decimals, sign, or junk. |
| `parse.amount_negative` | An amount is negative. | Amounts must be non-negative base-unit integers. |
| `parse.chain_malformed` | A chain id is not CAIP-2 (namespace:reference). | Use CAIP-2, e.g. "eip155:8453". |
| `parse.time_malformed` | A timestamp/duration is not a non-negative integer. | Use whole seconds as a non-negative integer. |
| `parse.time_negative` | A timestamp/duration is negative. | Durations must be non-negative whole seconds. |

## At payment time

You see these when the guard evaluates a payment — a verdict, or a problem parsing the server's challenge/authorization.

| Code | What it means | What to change |
|------|---------------|----------------|
| `adapter.concurrent_flow` | Two payment flows raced through one binding; refusing to mis-attribute. | Use one binding per concurrent flow; the refusal is a safety stop. |
| `adapter.context_incomplete` | The payment context lacked fields needed to evaluate it. | The adapter could not build a complete evaluation; the refusal is fail-closed. |
| `adapter.unguarded_signing_route` | A signing route that bypasses the veto was detected. | Route all signing through the guarded signer; the veto is the sole signing path. |
| `adapter.unsupported_x402_version` | The x402 payload version is not supported. | v1 handles the supported x402 generations; an unknown version is refused. |
| `allowlist.blocked` | The payee/chain is not on the allowlist. | Add this (address, chain) to policy.allowlist, or the block is correct. |
| `allowlist.empty` | No destinations are permitted (empty allowlist ⇒ deny all, secure by default). | Add at least one { address, chain } to policy.allowlist. |
| `bind.amount_mismatch` | The signed value does not equal the challenge amount. | Not a config error — the payment was tampered with or mismatched; the deny is correct. |
| `bind.asset_mismatch` | The signed (chain, token) does not equal the challenge's. | Not a config error — a mismatched payment; the deny is correct. |
| `bind.recipient_mismatch` | The signed recipient does not equal the challenge payTo. | Not a config error — a mismatched payment; the deny is correct. |
| `bind.timeout_exceeded` | The authorization's validBefore exceeds the allowed lifetime. | Raise policy.maxAuthLifetimeSeconds, or the server's window is too long; the deny is correct. |
| `cap.asset_unconfigured` | No caps are configured for this (asset, chain) denomination. | Add a policy.caps entry for this denomination — a missing entry denies by design. |
| `cap.global` | Cumulative spend in this denomination would exceed the global cap. | Raise caps.global, or the block is correct. |
| `cap.per_domain` | Cumulative spend to this domain would exceed the per-domain cap. | Raise caps.perDomain, or the block is correct. |
| `cap.per_request` | The amount exceeds the per-request cap. | Raise caps.perRequest for this denomination, or the block is correct. |
| `chain.unsupported` | The authorization form is not supported in v1 (EVM only). | v1 supports EVM only; SVM/other is v2. |
| `engine.error` | The policy engine threw unexpectedly; denying by default. | A bug — please report it. The guard fails closed rather than allow on an internal error. |
| `halt` | Kill switch engaged; all payments are denied. | Set policy.halt to false to resume. |
| `ok` | The payment passed every check. | None — this is an allow. |
| `origin.mismatch` | The challenge's resource origin does not match the request origin. | Expected when requireOriginMatch is on and origins differ; the deny is correct. |
| `parse.domain_malformed` | A domain is not a valid host. | Provide a bare host (no scheme, no port). |
| `parse.hex_malformed` | A value is not 0x-hex. | Provide 0x-prefixed hex. |
| `scheme.unsupported` | The payment scheme is not supported in v1 (exact only). | v1 supports the EVM exact scheme only; other schemes are refused. |
| `spend.contention` | The spend could not be committed within the retry budget under concurrent contention. | Transient under heavy multi-process contention; retry. Persistent ⇒ investigate the store. |
| `spend.record_failed` | The spend could not be durably recorded; denying rather than proceed uncounted. | Investigate ledger writability/disk; the guard fails closed. |
| `state.load_failed` | The spend state could not be loaded; denying by default. | Investigate the ledger directory (unreadable/corrupt); the guard fails closed. |
| `store.unverified` | The spend store failed its start-up atomicity self-test; the guard refuses to operate. | Investigate the ledger directory/filesystem; the guard fails closed rather than under-enforce. |
| `wire.unknown_v1_network` | A v1 network has no known CAIP-2 mapping. | The network is unrecognized; it cannot be keyed to caps or cross-checked. |
| `wire.unsupported_typed_data` | The EIP-712 typed data is not EIP-3009 TransferWithAuthorization. | v1 supports EVM exact (EIP-3009) only; other typed data is refused. |

## From a read API

You see these from a read-only method such as `snapshot()`.

| Code | What it means | What to change |
|------|---------------|----------------|
| `snapshot.state_unreadable` | snapshot() could not read spend state; it refuses to fabricate zeros. | Investigate the ledger; a zeroed snapshot would be a lie, so it fails loud. |
